description: Credit card rewards optimizer — upload transactions, get personalized card recommendations

userStories.title: Card catalog API with 20+ cards
userStories.description: D1-backed API serving credit card data. GET /api/cards returns full catalog, GET /api/cards/:id returns single card. Create the D1 database (card-optimizer-db and card-optimizer-db-staging), update wrangler.toml PLACEHOLDER IDs, create the schema migration, seed with 20+ real credit cards, and implement the Pages Functions endpoints. Also create the Cloudflare Pages project.
acceptanceCriteria: D1 databases created (card-optimizer-db and card-optimizer-db-staging) and wrangler.toml IDs updated
Cloudflare Pages project 'card-optimizer' created
GET /api/health returns 200 with healthy status
GET /api/cards returns JSON array of 20+ cards
Each card includes name, issuer, annualFee, rewardType, baseRate, categoryRates, signUpBonus, perks
GET /api/cards/:id returns single card or 404
API responses include Cache-Control headers
Database seeded with real credit card data (Chase, Amex, Citi, Capital One, Discover, Wells Fargo, etc.)
userStories.notes: See docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-12-card-optimizer.md in the ralph-bootstrap repo for detailed implementation including seed data for 20 cards. The plan is the source of truth for card data, schema, and API implementation.

userStories.title: CSV upload with auto-detection and spending breakdown
userStories.description: Client-side CSV parsing with Papa Parse. Auto-detects format from Monarch, Copilot, Chase, Amex, Capital One, and generic CSVs. Shows spending breakdown by reward category.
acceptanceCriteria: Drag-and-drop and file picker both work for CSV upload
Auto-detects CSV format and shows detected format name
Falls back to manual column mapping if auto-detect fails
Transactions categorized by merchant keyword matching
Spending breakdown table shows category, monthly avg, annual total, transaction count
Spending data persists in localStorage across page refreshes
No transaction data sent to server

userStories.title: Wallet optimization — per-category card assignment
userStories.description: User selects cards they hold from the catalog. System assigns each spending category to the best card and shows total projected rewards.
acceptanceCriteria: Card picker shows all cards grouped by issuer
Selected cards persist in localStorage
Per-category optimization shows which card to use where
Total annual reward and net value (minus fees) displayed
Updates in real-time as cards are added/removed

userStories.title: New card recommendations ranked by net annual value
userStories.description: Based on spending and current wallet, recommend new cards ranked by net annual value. Include sign-up bonus amortized over 2 years and category-level breakdown.
acceptanceCriteria: Recommendations exclude cards user already holds
Each recommendation shows net annual value, card name, issuer, reason
Category breakdown shows where the new card wins
Sign-up bonus amortized over 2 years included in calculation
Only cards with positive net value shown
Apply link for each recommended card

userStories.title: Wallet builder — optimal N-card combination
userStories.description: Find the optimal 2-4 card combination from the full catalog for the user's spending pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: User can select max cards (2, 3, or 4)
Shows optimal combination with net annual value
Lists each card in the optimal wallet with its role
Runs fast enough for 50 cards (under 2 seconds)

userStories.title: Card catalog browse page with search and filter
userStories.description: Searchable, filterable view of all cards in the database with full reward structure details.
acceptanceCriteria: Shows all cards in a grid layout
Search by card name
Filter by issuer
Each card shows: name, issuer, annual fee, reward rates, sign-up bonus, perks
Apply link on each card

userStories.title: Mobile responsive at 375px
userStories.description: All pages must be mobile-responsive with no horizontal overflow at 375px width.
acceptanceCriteria: No horizontal scroll on any page at 375px
Navigation is usable on mobile
Tables don't overflow on mobile
Tap targets are at least 44px
Text is readable without zooming

userStories.title: Share link with encoded state
userStories.description: Users can generate a share link that encodes their spending summary and wallet selection in the URL hash. No PII — just category totals and card IDs.
acceptanceCriteria: Share button generates URL with #share= hash
Opening shared URL restores spending summary and wallet selection
Share link contains no transaction-level data, only category totals
Invalid share links degrade gracefully (ignored, no crash)

userStories.title: SEO foundation — meta tags, OG, robots.txt, sitemap
userStories.description: Add essential SEO elements: meta description, Open Graph tags, Twitter card tags, canonical URL, robots.txt, sitemap.xml, and JSON-LD structured data. These are prerequisites for Google indexing and social sharing.
acceptanceCriteria: index.html has meta description (under 160 chars, mentions credit card rewards optimizer)
index.html has OG tags: og:title, og:description, og:type, og:url, og:image
index.html has Twitter card tags: twitter:card=summary_large_image, twitter:title, twitter:description
index.html has canonical URL link tag
public/robots.txt served correctly (not SPA fallback) — allows all crawlers
public/sitemap.xml lists all public routes (/, /upload, /wallet, /recommendations, /builder, /catalog)
JSON-LD structured data for WebApplication (name, url, description, applicationCategory)
OG image exists at public/og-image.png (1200x630, generated or placeholder)
Favicon exists (public/favicon.ico or public/favicon.svg)

userStories.title: Landing page with value proposition and how-it-works
userStories.description: Replace the / → /upload redirect with a real landing page. The landing page is the most important page for SEO and conversion. It must explain what the tool does, how it works, privacy guarantees, and have a clear CTA. Must render meaningful content in the initial HTML for search engines.
acceptanceCriteria: / route renders a landing page component (not a redirect to /upload)
Hero section: headline, subheadline, CTA button ('Optimize My Rewards' → /upload)
How It Works section: 3-step visual (Upload → Analyze → Optimize)
Features section: highlights key capabilities (wallet optimizer, recommendations, card catalog, share links)
Privacy section: prominent callout that all data stays in browser, no server upload, no tracking
Stats section: shows number of cards in database (fetched from /api/cards count)
Mobile responsive at 375px — no overflow, readable, tappable CTAs
CTA button visible above the fold on mobile
Visually polished — not a generic template look

userStories.title: Privacy policy and about page
userStories.description: Add a privacy policy page and footer with about/legal info. Essential for legitimacy of a financial tool. The privacy story is a selling point — all processing is client-side.
acceptanceCriteria: /privacy route renders a privacy policy page
Policy explains: no data sent to servers, localStorage only, no cookies, no tracking pixels
Policy states the tool is for informational purposes only, not financial advice
Footer added to all pages with links to Privacy and 'Built by Keylight Digital'
Mobile responsive

userStories.title: Push to GitHub and create README
userStories.description: Create scobb/card-optimizer GitHub repo with a polished README. GitHub is a distribution channel — developers searching for credit card optimization tools should find this.
acceptanceCriteria: GitHub repo scobb/card-optimizer created (public)
README.md with: project description, live demo link, features list, tech stack, screenshot or demo GIF placeholder, how to run locally, license
MIT LICENSE file added
.gitignore covers node_modules, dist, .wrangler
Code pushed to main branch on GitHub
Repo description set on GitHub

userStories.title: PWA manifest and installability
userStories.description: Add a web app manifest so the tool is installable as a PWA on mobile. Credit card optimization is a repeat-use tool — users should be able to add it to their home screen.
acceptanceCriteria: public/manifest.json with name, short_name, icons, start_url, display: standalone, theme_color
App icons at 192x192 and 512x512 (SVG-based or generated)
index.html links to manifest.json
index.html has theme-color meta tag
Chrome DevTools shows the app as installable (or Lighthouse PWA audit passes basic criteria)

userStories.title: Add Beam analytics
userStories.description: Integrate Beam analytics to track page views and usage patterns. We built Beam — we should use it. This gives us data on whether anyone is actually visiting.
acceptanceCriteria: Beam tracking script added to index.html
Site registered in Beam dashboard (cards.keylightdigital.dev)
Page views tracked on navigation between routes
No PII collected (Beam is privacy-first by design)
Script loads asynchronously — no impact on page load performance

userStories.title: Individual card detail pages for SEO
userStories.description: Create /cards/:slug routes that render detailed card pages. Each card page is an SEO target — 'Chase Sapphire Preferred review', 'Amex Gold rewards rate', etc. These pages should have unique meta tags and be listed in the sitemap.
acceptanceCriteria: /cards/:slug route renders a full detail page for each card
Card detail shows: name, issuer, annual fee, all reward rates by category, sign-up bonus, perks, pros/cons summary
Each card page has unique title and meta description
Sitemap updated to include all /cards/:slug routes
Back link to /catalog
'Add to Wallet' button that navigates to /wallet with card pre-selected
Mobile responsive at 375px

userStories.title: Spending category guides for SEO content
userStories.description: Create /best-cards/:category pages (e.g., /best-cards/dining, /best-cards/groceries, /best-cards/travel). These are high-intent SEO pages targeting 'best credit card for X' searches. Content is generated from the card database.
acceptanceCriteria: /best-cards/:category route renders a guide page
Pages exist for at least: dining, groceries, travel, gas, online-shopping, streaming
Each page lists top 5 cards for that category ranked by reward rate
Each card entry shows: name, category rate, annual fee, net value estimate
Unique title and meta description per category page
Sitemap includes all category guide pages
Internal links between guide pages and card detail pages
Mobile responsive at 375px
